text: This study seeks to determine the contributions and challenges of theological education in TACN from 1931 till date. The study also seeks to investigate the Apostolic institutions imparting theological knowledge within TACN and also to examine the extent to which these institutions have impacted theological instruction and pastoral ministry. In addition, it seeks to identify the challenges TACN is facing as it strives to sustain theological education. The research methodology employed in this study was historical and qualitative. Hence, the researcher used method of document analysis and interviews with some staff and students of the Apostolic seminaries. The source of data was analysed using thematic analysis. Findings of this study revealed that The Apostolic Church of Nigeria (TACN) has contributed significantly to the intellectual and spiritual capacity building of its leaders which has positively impacted negatively and positively on the doctrine of the church and also positively on the growth of the church. It also revealed that the institutions that were established by the church in other to train its clergy, namely The Apostolic Church Theological Seminary in Amumara and The Apostolic Church LAWNA Theological Seminary in Jos have helped to structure the training of clerics in the church. However, the study revealed that the church is still facing many challenges such as poor funding, traditional believes and attitude towards Western education, the need to reconcile spiritual development and academic proficiency. The study therefore concluded that in order for the church to sustain its programme of theological education, there is the need for improvement in facilities, in ICT facilities and staff capacity building. In addition, it was recommended that the Church should also strive to contextualize its theological training so as to meet the realities of the time. Also, that the Apostolic church should endeavour to make theological training holistic by introducing scholarly work and spiritual training to the clergy of the church.
explanation: The text uses formal academic phrasing with a structured abstract, but contains several awkward phrases and repetition that can be indicative of AI-generated content, suggesting a moderate likelihood of AI authorship.
